name: Chocolate Maple Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Mary M McBride's Encyclopedia of Cooking
Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly grease two 9-inch layer pans.
Sift flour once before measuring, then sift baking powder, salt and baking soda 3 times.
Cream butter until soft and smooth. Gradually add sugar, creaming until light and fluffy.
Add beaten egg yolks, then melted chocolate. Add milk alternately with dry ingredients,
beating until smooth after each addition. Continue beating for two minutes.
Fold in vanilla extract and stiffly beaten egg whites. Pour into prepared pans and bake for about 30 minutes.
Turn out onto a wire rack to cool. Split layers horizontally.
To prepare frosting, cream butter until soft. Slowly stir in powdered sugar and salt.
Add additional sugar alternately with cream, beating thoroughly after each addition until creamy and smooth.
Stir in walnuts with the last of the sugar. Beat in maple flavoring. If needed, add cream for spreading consistency.
Spread between layers, then frost top and sides of cake.
